name: Pitta Me Kima (Chopped Meat Pie)
recipeIngredient: or 1 tablespoon Tomato paste diluted with:
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the meat, half the butter, and onions in a large pot, stirring with a wooden spoon to break up the meat.
2. Add tomatoes (or diluted tomato paste) cinnamon, parsley, salt, and pepper. Simmer until all liquid is absorbed (about 30 minutes).
3. Soak the toast in milk, then mash it with a fork.
4. Remove the cinnamon stick and add the milk-toast mixture to the meat; remove from heat.
5. Add cheese and eggs, and mix well.
6. Melt the remaining butter; grease a pan about 2 inches smaller than the phyllo.
7. Place 7 to 8 phyllo sheets into the pan, greasing each before adding it, then let extend on all sides.
8. Pour in the meat mixture and spread evenly.
9. Fold the overlapping phyllo back onto the meat; grease these well.
10. Carefully cut remaining sheets of phyllo to fit the top of the pan. Brush each with butter and lay on the filling to make the top of the pitta.
11. Pour any remaining butter over the top and sprinkle lightly with water (to keep the phyllo from rising too high).
12. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 30 to 40 minutes.
13. Cool about 30 minutes, then cut into squares to serve.
